claim: WHO IHR monitoring reports that fewer than 50 percent of low-income countries meet core surveillance capacity requirements, with reporting timeliness below 30 percent in several countries.

source.title: IHR (2005) States Parties Self-Assessment Annual Reporting (SPAR) — WHO

method: Reviewed IHR monitoring data, surveillance-capacity indicators, and timeliness metrics.
limitations: Self-assessment data may not reflect actual capacity.
Timeliness metrics vary by disease and reporting system.
Source URL repaired June 2026; the surveillance-capacity figure should be reconfirmed against current e-SPAR analytics.
